params.name: Wheatstone Bridge
params.description:
On Jul 4, 1833 AD, British inventor Samuel Christie invented the Wheatstone bridge.
The Wheatstone Bridge is an instrument that can accurately measure resistance. It uses a bridge circuit made up of four resistors and measures changes in electrical current to determine changes in physical quantity. It is a highly accurate method of measurement. The Wheatstone Bridge is not named after its inventor because Wheatstone was the first to use it to measure resistance, so people became accustomed to …
